steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 argues that genre classification is unhelpful — genres are "ambiguous, subjective, and simplistic." The question asks how Text 1's author would respond to Costa's claim that genre classification benefits listeners.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — By emphasizing that humans do not necessarily enjoy every recording in a genre they claim to prefer: Raises concerns about listener preferences — a topic Text 1 does not discuss. ✗
  • Choice B — By asserting that genre classifications may not be the most helpful way to think about music: Argues that improving a flawed framework does not make it useful — directly consistent with Text 1's skepticism that genre classification is a poor framework to begin with. ✓
  • Choice C — By arguing that some genres are more easily recognized by classification systems than others: Treats genre classification as fundamentally sound, contradicting Text 1. ✗
  • Choice D — By suggesting that future research may provide substantial advancements in the field of automated genre classification: Also treats genre classification as valuable, contradicting Text 1. ✗
B is correct.
